name: Épila and Santuario de Rodanas Loop
name_original: Épila, Azucarera del Jalón y Santuario de Rodanas — gravel circular desde Limpiaque
description: Explore the Epila, Azucarera del Jalon, and Santuario de Rodanas on this 22.2-mile moderate gravel cycling route from Limpiaque.
region_intro: Cycling routes Lumpiaque Spain are characterized by the fertile plains along the Jalón River, offering a blend of cultural heritage and accessible natural features. The landscape primarily consists of extensive fruit orchards, providing a pleasant and relatively flat backdrop for touring cyclists. Routes often feature a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with some gentle undulations in elevation. This region provides diverse terrain suitable for various touring cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Lumpiaque The most popular touring cycling route is Épila and Santuario de Rodanas Loop, a 22.2 miles (35.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route features undulating terrain and passes by cultural landmarks. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bike loop from Rueda de Jalón-Lumpiaque, an easy 11.9 miles (19.1 km) path. This route offers flat terrain along the riverside, winding through fruit orchards. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Urrea de Jalón, a 13.0 miles (21.0 km) trail leading through riverside landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Touring cycling around Lumpiaque is defined by the Jalón River, extensive fruit orchards, and varied terrain including both paved and unpaved sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside loops to more challenging routes with greater elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lumpiaque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Lumpiaque?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Lumpiaque, catering to various skill levels. These routes offer a diverse experience, from easy riverside loops to more challenging rides with greater elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Lumpiaque?
A: The terrain around Lumpiaque is quite varied. You'll find routes that meander through the fertile plains along the Jalón River, often featuring extensive fruit orchards. Many routes offer a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with some gentle undulations in elevation, providing a diverse cycling experience.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Lumpiaque offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Rueda de Jalón-Lumpiaque&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 11.9-mile (19.1 km) path that offers flat terrain along the riverside, winding through fruit orchards. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38165061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Urrea de Jalón&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.0-mile (21.0 km) trail through similar riverside landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Lumpiaque area?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Lumpiaque are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994014131/epila-and-santuario-de-rodanas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Épila and Santuario de Rodanas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 22.2-mile (35.7 km) route, and the easier &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Rueda de Jalón-Lumpiaque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What cultural landmarks or attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Lumpiaque's routes often pass by significant cultural landmarks. You can visit the magnificent 15th-century Mudéjar tower of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5324587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of San Francisco de Asís of Lumpiaque&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3155414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Rodanas&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994014131/epila-and-santuario-de-rodanas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Épila and Santuario de Rodanas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Mayor of Épila&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that showcase the fruit orchards or the Jalón River?
A: Absolutely. The fertile plain along the Jalón River is a defining feature of the region, and many routes are designed to highlight this. You'll find extensive fruit orchards providing a picturesque backdrop for your ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Rueda de Jalón-Lumpiaque&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, winding directly through these agricultural landscapes.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Lumpiaque?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's climate, characterized by fertile plains and orchards, suggests spring and autumn would be ideal. These seasons typically offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, avoiding the intense heat of summer or potential chill of winter.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a greater challenge, there are routes with more significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31853756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Épila loop from Rueda de Jalón-Lumpiaque&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 34.2-mile (55.0 km) route with over 570 meters of elevation gain, offering a more demanding experience.

Q: What are some unique natural features to explore while cycling in Lumpiaque?
A: Beyond the orchards and river, a unique natural attraction is the "Balsa and Bodegón." This recreational area features a pond fed by an underground spring and an artificial cave, possibly Roman in origin, with tunnels leading to the subterranean water source. While not directly on a cycling path, it offers a tranquil stop and a glimpse into the region's historical use of water resources.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Lumpiaque?
A: Over 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lumpiaque's varied terrain. While specific reviews aren't detailed here, the popularity indicates that cyclists appreciate the mix of cultural heritage, accessible natural features, and diverse routes, from easy riverside loops to more challenging options.

Q: Are there options for shorter touring cycling trips in Lumpiaque?
A: Yes, Lumpiaque offers several shorter touring cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Rueda de Jalón-Lumpiaque&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 11.9-mile (19.1 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38165061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Urrea de Jalón&lt;/a&gt; is a 13.0-mile (21.0 km) trail, both suitable for shorter outings.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the cycling routes in Lumpiaque?
A: Lumpiaque is a municipality with a local community, so you can expect to find cafes, pubs, and restaurants within the town itself. While specific establishments along the routes are not detailed, planning your ride to pass through Lumpiaque or nearby villages will provide opportunities for refreshments and meals.
tips: The beautiful town of Épila has a lot to teach you with how small it is. Apart from natural and scenic attractions worth visiting, we must also highlight the Condes de Aranda Palace (from the 16th century), the Marquises of Saudi or the Condes de Montenegrón building. If we also like to visit religious heritage, the town of the Valdejalón region has the Church of Santa María la Mayor or the hermitage of Santa María Magdalena

A remnant of Industrial Heritage, a reminder of the days when the sugar beet industry was a booming business. The neighborhood that surrounds it is part of the original infrastructure of the factory. It is next to the beginning of the path that leads to the Sanctuary of Rodanas.

http://www.sipca.es/censo/1-INM-ZAR-016-099-108/Azucarera/del/Jal%C3%B3n.html#.X0KIcTXtY2w

Located in a mountain area far from all the surrounding towns, the Sanctuary is made up of a church, the parish house, the house of the santero and a guesthouse. Around it grew a neighborhood of small houses still inhabited. Next to the Sanctuary there is a small restaurant that, without much sophistication, can be used to have a drink in the middle of the route. There are also tables and barbecues where (if restrictions allow) you can make your own food.

The road from Epila has recently been paved. It is about 15 km with about 300 meters of unevenness, so it is a bit difficult in the direction of the Sanctuary but it is very easy in the direction of Epila. As it is a paved rural road, it has little traffic, mainly from local vehicles and especially on weekends.

Once in the Sanctuary, you can make MTB routes in the surroundings, taking advantage of the unevenness of the surrounding mountains, and its pine forests (partly devastated two decades ago by a strong fire).

Starting from Epila, it is an easy and not very demanding route of about 30 km that, although it crosses a large plain with few emotions, has the reward of arriving in the wooded area and the picnic area. Perfect for a family outing.

http://www.sipca.es/censo/1-INM-ZAR-016-099-004/Santuario/de/Nuestra/Se%C3%B1ora/de/Rodanas.html#.X0KJETXtY2w

As we pass through the beautiful town of Lumpiaque belonging to the Valdejalón region, we can visit the parish church of San Francisco de Asís, built in the 16th century with a beautiful Mudejar bell tower, currently listed as a World Heritage Site; or also the Hermitage of Santa Bárbara, dating from the 17th century.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this gravel circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 35.7 km long and typically takes around 2 hours and 48 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of gravel roads, making it ideal for gravel bikes. You can expect a mix of wide tracks, some potentially technical sections, and possibly some paved segments. The scenery is diverse, featuring mountains, ravines, extensive pine forests, and ancient olive groves, with cultivated fields in lower sections.

faqs.question: Is this gravel route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate in difficulty. While some segments in the broader area are described as 'easy' or 'suitable for all fitness levels', the full circular route includes an elevation gain of over 320 meters, suggesting it's best for cyclists with some experience. A longer version of a similar route is described as 'demanding but spectacular'.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable landmarks. These include the Church of Santa María la Mayor of Épila, the historical Jalón Sugar Factory, and the picturesque Sanctuary of Rodanas, which is set in the foothills of the Iberian System. The Parish Church of San Francisco de Asís of Lumpiaque is also a highlight.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route in Limpiaque?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting from Limpiaque, you can typically find parking within the town itself. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but look for public parking options near the presumed starting point in Limpiaque.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Aragon region can experience hot summers. For gravel cycling, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions. These seasons provide comfortable weather for enjoying the diverse landscapes without extreme heat or cold.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no specific information regarding dog restrictions on this particular gravel route. However, when cycling with dogs, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife, livestock, or other trail users, and to ensure they are well-behaved and don't disturb the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for cycling this route or accessing the natural areas it traverses. Access to the Sanctuary of Rodanas is generally open to visitors.

faqs.question: Are there any traffic-free sections on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of the route, such as the segment from the Sanctuary of Rodanas towards Lucena de Jalón, are noted for being largely traffic-free. This enhances the safety and enjoyment for cyclists, allowing for a more immersive experience in nature.

faqs.question: What kind of natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse natural beauty, including mountains, ravines, and extensive pine forests. Near the Sanctuary of Rodanas, you'll find pine, holm oak, savin, and blackthorn trees, along with ancient olive groves. Lower sections may also feature cultivated fields, providing a varied landscape throughout your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through towns like Épila and Lumpiaque, specific cafes or pubs directly on the trail are not detailed. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, and plan stops in the towns for refreshments or meals.
